skill.name: building-skills
skill.description: Guides creation, validation, and maintenance of Claude Code skills, including SKILL.md frontmatter, directory structure, allowed tools, templates, and safety checks.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and maintain Claude Code skills with templates and safety guidance.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the weak-crypto and Ruby backtick alerts are false positives from Markdown examples, validation text, and product terminology. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill pre-approves Write, Edit, and Bash for a community skill and includes a local validator script that reads user-supplied paths. No evidence found of network access, credential exfiltration, destructive commands, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: Broad Pre-Approved File and Shell Permissions
medium_findings.description: The skill metadata pre-approves Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob, and Bash. This matches the skill creation workflow, but it gives a community skill direct ability to modify files and run local commands when invoked.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools frontmatter directly grants Write, Edit, and Bash. The intent appears legitimate for creating skills, so this is a permission risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Local Validator Reads User-Supplied Paths
low_findings.description: The validation script accepts a skill directory argument, reads SKILL.md, lists subdirectories, and checks script permissions. This is expected validator behavior, but it should only be run on paths the user intended to inspect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access is clear and local-only. No network transfer or secret harvesting logic was found, making this a minor operational risk.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged many weak cryptography locations, but review found no hashing, encryption, crypto imports, or password handling. The flagged lines are documentation about Claude Code skills, model fields, descriptions, or YAML examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain prose, frontmatter validation, and examples, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms being used.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Mostly Inert
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown code spans, fenced examples, slash-command examples, and paths. They document maintenance workflows and are not executable code in the skill files themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious command strings are inside Markdown documentation. They could guide an assistant to run commands, but they are not hidden execution logic or injected shell code.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Malicious Override Text
low_findings.description: Review looked for override language such as ignoring prior instructions, fake system updates, pre-approval claims, or administrator authority. No evidence found beyond normal discussion of pre-approved allowed tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Suspicious prompt-injection phrases were not found. The only pre-approval language describes Claude Code allowed-tools behavior, not a request to bypass marketplace review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Permission in Skill Frontmatter
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash in allowed-tools, enabling command execution when the skill is trusted and invoked.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permission is directly present in frontmatter. It is useful for validation workflows but increases execution risk for a community skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Write and Edit Permissions in Skill Frontmatter
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Write and Edit in allowed-tools, enabling filesystem changes while creating or maintaining skills.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permissions are explicitly listed. They are aligned with the skill purpose but should be reviewed before publication.

content.value_statement: Creating reliable AI skills requires correct frontmatter, clear triggers, safe permissions, and useful resources. This skill provides templates, validation guidance, and maintenance rules for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use a skill instead of an agent or command.
Defines valid SKILL.md frontmatter and directory structure.
Provides reusable skill templates and example sections.
Documents allowed-tools formatting and permission tradeoffs.
Includes a Python validator for local skill directory checks.
Guides maintenance tasks such as removing unsupported model fields.
limitations: Does not publish skills to any marketplace by itself.
Does not verify runtime safety of arbitrary generated helper scripts.
Requires careful review before granting Bash, Write, or Edit permissions.
Focuses on Claude Code skill conventions, not every AI platform format.

use_cases.title: Create a New Skill
use_cases.description: Design a new Claude Code skill with clear triggers, valid frontmatter, and a practical resource layout.

use_cases.title: Validate Existing Skills
use_cases.description: Check SKILL.md files for schema issues, naming problems, unsupported model fields, and resource references.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Automation
use_cases.description: Create consistent skill templates and permission practices for shared AI development workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Claude Code skill for [domain]. Include a clear name, description, required frontmatter, and a simple directory structure.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Invocation Triggers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md description and make the auto-invocation triggers more specific without exceeding the recommended length.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Allowed Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate the allowed-tools field for this skill. Recommend the minimum safe tool set and explain any risks from Bash, Write, or Edit.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Complete Skill Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete skill package for [workflow]. Include SKILL.md sections, scripts, references, templates, validation steps, and maintenance guidance.

output_examples.input: I need a skill that helps write API documentation.
output: Recommended skill name and invocation description.
A SKILL.md outline with capabilities, use triggers, and examples.
Suggested references for style rules and API documentation patterns.

output_examples.input: Check whether my skill frontmatter is valid.
output: Validation notes for required fields and naming conventions.
Warnings about unsupported model fields or incorrect allowed-tools format.
Suggested corrections that preserve the skill purpose.

output_examples.input: Help my team standardize skills across a repo.
output: A reusable template structure for new skills.
A review checklist for permissions, resources, and invocation clarity.

best_practices: Start with the smallest allowed-tools set that supports the workflow.
Write descriptions that state exactly when the skill should be invoked.
Run validation and review generated scripts before sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not add a model field to SKILL.md frontmatter.
Do not grant Bash, Write, or Edit when read-only guidance is enough.
Do not use vague descriptions that make invocation unpredictable.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create and maintain Claude Code skills with valid frontmatter, useful documentation, and practical resource folders.

faq.question: Can it validate an existing skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance and a local Python validator for SKILL.md structure, naming, descriptions, and allowed-tools format.

faq.question: Does it require Bash permission?
faq.answer: The current skill declares Bash because validation and setup workflows may use commands. Review this permission before enabling it.

faq.question: Is this only for Claude Code?
faq.answer: It is designed around Claude Code skill conventions, but many planning and documentation practices also help Codex workflows.

faq.question: Does it create secure helper scripts automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and validation checks, but any generated helper script still needs human security review.

faq.question: When should I use an agent instead of a skill?
faq.answer: Use an agent for explicit isolated tasks. Use a skill for always-on expertise that should activate from context.
